article.description: Fred Gentry's 18 points and game-high 14 rebounds led McNeese State to a 65-43 victory over Louisiana-Monroe in the Southland Conference tournament title game Saturday for its first NCAA tournament berth in 13 years.
article.headline: Victory sweet for Cowboys, who end 13-year wait
article.linkText: McNeese St. avoids last year's fate, wins Southland
article.story:
LAKE CHARLES, La. (AP) -- Fred Gentry's 18 points and game-high
14 rebounds led McNeese State to a 65-43 victory over
Louisiana-Monroe in the Southland Conference tournament title game
Saturday for its first NCAA tournament berth in 13 years.

	   McNeese State's first-year coach, Tic Price -- who resigned from
Memphis in 1999 because of an affair with a student -- cried as he
and his wife embraced on the sideline after Saturday's game.

	   The Cowboys (21-8) led only 25-22 at halftime, but dominated the
final 20 minutes, shooting 60 percent (15-for-25) while nearly
doubling the Indians' point total.

	   Gentry dominated inside, either drawing fouls or hitting bank
shots en route to shooting 6-of-11 from the field and 6-of-9 from
the free-throw line. He had 10 rebounds at the defensive end.

	   Ben Perkins added 14 points, and Damond Williams 11 for McNeese,
which shot 40 percent from the field.

	   Wojciech Myrda led Louisiana-Monroe (20-12) with 13 points,
while Kirby Lemons scored 11. The Indians shot only 32 percent.

	   Price was a rising star in the coaching ranks when he went 67-23
at New Orleans in the mid-1990s. He then went to Memphis, where he
was 30-27 before leaving.

	   Price moved to McNeese as an assistant and was promoted last
offseason when Ron Everhart left for Northeastern.
